name: corellm
description: Monitor LiteLLM proxy balances, budgets, and usage — status bar spend tracking, rich dashboards, key health, team budgets, model pricing, CSV/PDF export

corellm.endpoint.markdownDescription:
The LiteLLM proxy base URL. Include the scheme (http/https) and port if non-standard.

Examples:
- `http://localhost:4000` (local dev)
- `https://llm-proxy.example.com` (production)

corellm.apiKey.markdownDescription:
Your LiteLLM API key — the key whose balance you want to monitor. Used as the `Authorization: Bearer` header.

Leave empty if using **Username + Password** login instead.

corellm.adminKey.markdownDescription:
LiteLLM proxy master key (admin). Required to query other keys, list all keys, and access admin-only endpoints.

**Auth priority:** login-derived key → adminKey → apiKey.

corellm.username.markdownDescription:
LiteLLM UI username for login-based authentication. When set together with **Password**, the extension calls `/login`, extracts the session key, and uses it for all subsequent requests.

This is the preferred auth method for LiteLLM UI deployments.

corellm.password.description: LiteLLM UI password for login-based authentication. Used together with Username.

corellm.keyToQuery.markdownDescription:
A specific API key ID to query balance and spend for — can be different from the key used for authentication.

Use this when you want to monitor a **different key** than the one you're authenticated with. Leave empty to monitor your own key.

corellm.teamFilter.markdownDescription:
Limit all panels and data to a single team ID. When set, only spend, budgets, and keys belonging to this team are shown.

Leave empty to see all teams (requires admin privileges).

corellm.refreshInterval.markdownDescription:
How often (in seconds) CoreLLM polls the LiteLLM API for updated balance and spend data.

- Minimum: 5 seconds
- Default: 60 seconds
- Set to `0` to **disable auto-refresh** completely (manual refresh only via the refresh command or clicking the status bar).

corellm.cacheResults.markdownDescription:
Cache API responses in memory for the duration of the polling interval. Reduces network requests and speeds up panel switching.

Disable if you need real-time data on every interaction.

enumDescriptions: Cycle through all modes on each click
Usage % with an ASCII progress bar: [████░░░░] 65%
corellm.statusBarDisplayMode.markdownDescription:
What information to show in the VS Code status bar.

Click the status bar item or use **CoreLLM: Cycle Status Bar Display** to rotate through modes at any time.

corellm.compactStatusBar.markdownDescription:
Use a minimal status bar display showing only the dollar amount (e.g., `$4.32`). Saves status bar space.

When disabled, the full label is shown (e.g., `$(credit-card) my-key: $4.32 / $100.00`).

corellm.showKeyAlias.description: Include the key alias or key name alongside the balance in the status bar.

corellm.showSpendLogs.markdownDescription:
Fetch and display recent individual spend entries in the status bar tooltip (from `/spend/logs`).

Enable this to see per-request costs directly from the status bar hover.

corellm.showTeamSpend.description: Append team-level spend breakdown to the status bar tooltip.

corellm.showGlobalSpend.description: Append global (proxy-wide) spend totals to the status bar tooltip.

corellm.enableTreeView.markdownDescription:
Show the CoreLLM Monitor in the VS Code sidebar. Provides quick-access buttons for all commands.

Disable if you prefer using only the status bar and command palette.

corellm.treeViewAutoRefresh.description: Keep the sidebar tree view data in sync with the polling interval.

enumDescriptions: Budget Overview — key balance, spend charts, budget gauge
corellm.defaultPanelTab.description: Which tab opens first when launching a dashboard panel.

corellm.webviewTheme.description: Color theme for all CoreLLM webview panels. 'vscode' automatically matches your editor theme.

corellm.showModelSpend.description: Show per-model cost breakdown (gpt-4, claude-3, etc.) inside dashboard panels.

corellm.enableProviderBreakdown.description: Show per-provider spend breakdown (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.) in dashboard views.

corellm.dateFormat.description: How dates and timestamps are displayed inside webview panels.

corellm.reportDuration.markdownDescription:
Default time window for spend reports, charts, and budget overviews.

You can temporarily override this with the **CoreLLM: Set Report Duration** command.

corellm.reportCustomStart.description: Custom report start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Only used when reportDuration is set to 'custom'.

corellm.reportCustomEnd.description: Custom report end date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Only used when reportDuration is set to 'custom'.

corellm.enableHealthMonitoring.markdownDescription:
Continuously monitor proxy health by polling `/health` endpoints. Detects unhealthy model deployments and shows them in the **Health Dashboard**.

Enable this if you want proactive alerts when models go down.

corellm.healthCheckInterval.markdownDescription:
How often (in seconds) to check proxy health. Only relevant when **Enable Health Monitoring** is on.

- Minimum: 30 seconds
- Default: 300 seconds (5 minutes)

corellm.healthAlertOnUnhealthy.markdownDescription: Show a VS Code popup notification when any model endpoint transitions to **unhealthy**. The notification includes the model name and endpoint URL.

corellm.enableActivityMonitoring.markdownDescription:
Fetch proxy-wide activity data from `/global/activity` endpoints. Adds request counts, model usage, and exception tracking to the Activity panel.

⚠️ May increase API load on the proxy.

corellm.enableSpendForecast.markdownDescription:
Project future spend in the Unified Dashboard based on your recent usage patterns. Uses a linear projection over the lookback window.

Disable if you only want to see historical data.

corellm.forecastDays.markdownDescription:
Number of recent days to use as the basis for spend forecasting. A longer window smooths out daily spikes; a shorter window is more responsive to recent changes.

- 1 day: very sensitive to today's usage
- 7 days: balanced (default)
- 30 days: stable, long-term trend

corellm.budgetWarningThreshold.markdownDescription:
**Remaining budget** warning threshold (percentage of `max_budget`).

When the key's remaining budget falls below this percent, the status bar turns orange/yellow.

Example: with `max_budget = $100` and threshold `20`, you get a warning when you have less than $20 left.

Set to `0` to disable this warning.

corellm.budgetAlertThreshold.markdownDescription:
**Budget usage** alert threshold (percentage of `max_budget` consumed).

When the key has spent more than this percent of its budget, a VS Code notification fires. This is the **spend-side** counterpart to `budgetWarningThreshold` (which is remaining-side).

Example: `max_budget = $100`, threshold `80` → alert when you've spent $80+.

Only active when `enableBudgetAlerts` is on.

corellm.enableBudgetAlerts.description: Show VS Code popup notifications when any key's budget usage exceeds the budgetAlertThreshold.

corellm.spendAlertThreshold.markdownDescription:
**Per-request** spend alert in USD. When any single API call costs more than this amount, a VS Code notification fires.

Useful for catching accidental expensive requests (e.g., a single call that costs $5+).

Set to `0` to disable.

corellm.enableDailySpendAlert.description: Show a VS Code notification when today's total spend exceeds the daily spend threshold.

corellm.dailySpendAlertThreshold.markdownDescription:
Daily spend alert limit in USD. When today's total spend exceeds this amount, a notification fires.

Only active when `enableDailySpendAlert` is on. Set to `0` to disable.

corellm.enableRequestLogging.markdownDescription:
Log raw HTTP request and response bodies for debugging. View captured logs with the **CoreLLM: Show Request Logs** command.

⚠️ May contain API keys and sensitive data. Disable in production.

scripts.test: npm run compile && npm run lint
